3	Stefan Georgi
MODULE 3	Mechanism Overview
Unique
Mechanism
(00:02)
Now, we’re going move on to unique mechanisms in this section. We’ve
covered research R, and then the mechanisms is the M in RMBC. Mechanisms
aren’t really that complicated, but they’re really important. It’s really crucial
that you have this component, if you want to write the best sales copy or have
the best marketing and messaging and everything like that. In some respects
unique mechanisms are kind of like a unique differentiator or a unique selling
proposition or unique value proposition. All of those sorts of things are kind
of tied in to unique mechanism. Let me just kind of read through this with you
and then I may extrapolate or expand or expound or pontificate from there.
In a nutshell, unique mechanism explains why our prospect is struggling
or suffering to achieve a goal and how they can overcome that problem or
overcome their struggles.
(01:05)
At the core, a unique mechanism always has two parts. It’s always a two
part logical connection. There’s a unique mechanism behind the problem
our prospect faces. There’s a unique mechanism behind the solution to that
problem. What’s really important is that we identified the unique mechanism
behind both the problem and the solution. This is where I guess my contribution
to this idea kind of comes from. I didn’t come up with the idea of a unique
mechanism 	at 	all. 	People 	have 	been 	talking 	about 	mechanisms 	behind
mechanisms of action, mechanisms of problems, things like that, but it’s
really the combining of the two. That’s super important. I think that’s where
people hear about mechanisms, but they don’t have this part. They’re missing
this part, then this is where they get tripped up. Why do we want to do that?
(01:57)
Why do we want to combine both? Well, by showing the unique mechanism
behind the problem, you get the prospect to understand why they failed in
the past. By showing the unique mechanism behind the solution, you get
www.getwsodo.com	www.getwsodo.com

-- 6 of 9 --

4	Stefan Georgi
MODULE 3	Mechanism Overview
the prospect to believe that our product will bring them success when other
alternatives to fail. Then this part here is pretty, I’m going to just kind of verbally
talk about it versus read this all to you, but basically what this part says, and
you’ll have this as part of the course, is you have to think about it like this. By
the time a prospect comes to you, they’ve tried other solutions. It’s very, very
rare that whatever your solution is, it’s like the first time that they’ve tried to
solve this problem or the first time they’ve heard of your solution on a broad
sense.
(02:48)
Because if you are selling dieting or exercise or annuities or income investing
or managing for profit, any of those sorts of things. Whatever it is you’re
selling a car, whatever it is you’re selling, they’ve been sold that before. They’ve
probably bought things that are similar to your product before as well. There’s
always going to be a degree of skepticism right away. Or a question of like,
is this similar to what I’ve tried in the past? And if it is, why will my results
be any different today if I haven’t had the results I wanted in the past. They
haven’t had the results they want in the past where they wouldn’t be engaging
with you, whether it’s through your sales letter or a sales meeting or whatever
it is. They have a pain point, but they’re looking for something that’s going
to actually solve the pain point and they’re wary or skeptical because they
haven’t succeeded in the past.
(03:51)
That’s why we need to have this unique mechanism. We need to differentiate.
We have to show them, well actually with the unique mechanism of the
problem, we just show them actually, here’s the real reason why you fail in
the past. You’ve tried these other solutions, but they were all missing this
one piece. They all got this one thing wrong. You tried Keto, but it turns out
that here’s the one thing about here about Keto that nobody tells you the one
problem of Keto. The one reason Keto doesn’t work for you and maybe it’s
hormones or your metabolism or really the unique or the better of what you
can find through research. I’m going to do a bonus module on this from a
health perspective. I’m going to show you some other mechanism examples
here as well.
(04:35)
That’s like the missing 1%. It’s like one, and you see down here, I bold one
crucial piece of information. That’s super, super important because what we
www.getwsodo.com	www.getwsodo.com

-- 7 of 9 --

5	Stefan Georgi
MODULE 3	Mechanism Overview
want them to understand, it’s like, okay, you were really close before maybe
that’s why you got partial results but not complete results because a lot of
times people have. You were close before, but again, there’s this one missing
piece of information. This 1%, you had 99% of it, but there was 1% you were
missing and that’s the reason why you didn’t succeed. And if you do that and
we can show them how our product or service or whatever incorporates that
missing 1%, then we have them in a really good place because if you get them
to believe that really this just was one thing they were missing and then you
can tell them, I will give you that missing one percent.
(05:27)
Either I’ll do it for you or you can buy it or whenever it is. Then as long as they
really believe you, it’s easy for them to latch onto the solution that you’re
offering them and really believe that it will work, which makes it a lot easier for
them to buy. If they believe that your solution really works, they’ll buy. That’s
the biggest secret of all with copywriting in general. It’s selling actually period
is like if somebody has a pain point and they actually 100% believed it’s a bad
enough pain point and they believed truly 100% that if they gave you money,
that pain point would go away, then they would do it almost every time. Your
conversion rates would be 50% 90% 70% of the time you would convert.
(06:15)
Does it make sense? The truth is they don’t, a lot of them don’t, which is why
for online with like 50 or $100 price product, your actual conversion rate is
maybe anywhere from one to 5%. So one to 5% of the time through all of this
copy, you can get somebody to believe you enough to buy from you. But the
higher you can get that believability factor, the more you can get them to
believe you, the higher your conversion rates going to be. Again, they’ve been
burned, they’ve seen other solutions. So we have to differentiate both the
problem and the solution. That’s why the unique mechanism is so vital. I hope
that makes sense. Obviously, like I said, we got to look at some examples.
Let’s go ahead and do that. We’ll jump in and in the next couple of videos I’ll
be going through examples of unique mechanisms behind the problem and
unique mechanisms behind the solution.
